title: National Industrial IoT Platform
entry_mechanism: SDAIA-Aramco-STC consortium developing national IIoT platform with defence and industrial use cases
executive_summary:
Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) — connecting industrial equipment, sensors, and systems to cloud platforms for monitoring, analytics, and control — is the digital transformation foundation for Saudi Arabia's manufacturing, energy, and utilities sectors. Saudi Arabia's Aramco, SABIC, Saudi Electricity Company, and Ma'aden collectively operate millions of industrial sensors and control points, most of which feed data into disparate foreign-operated platforms.

Node DG-5 targets a Saudi-developed National Industrial IoT Platform: a standardised middleware, device management, and analytics layer that allows Saudi industrial companies to connect their equipment to a Saudi-operated platform with Saudi data sovereignty. The platform serves as the industrial data fabric for Vision 2030's manufacturing ambition — every factory in the Saudi industrial clusters (KAEC, Jubail, Yanbu) connected to a single Saudi IIoT fabric.

The global IIoT platform market was valued at $108 billion in 2024 growing at 23.9% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's industrial base — one of the world's largest petrochemical and energy sectors — creates a massive domestic IIoT platform demand. A Saudi IIoT platform with Aramco and SABIC as reference customers has strong GCC and MENA export potential.

companies.name: Siemens MindSphere / Xcelerator
companies.rationale: Siemens Xcelerator is a leading IIoT platform with active Saudi Arabia industrial deployments. Technology partnership for Saudi-operated deployment.
companies.contact: Siemens VP Digital Industries, Saudi Arabia

companies.name: PTC (ThingWorx)
companies.rationale: PTC ThingWorx is a leading IIoT platform with Saudi Arabia enterprise customers. Technology licensing for Saudi-operated platform.

companies.name: Microsoft Azure IoT
companies.rationale: Azure IoT Hub is widely deployed in Saudi industrial companies. Saudi sovereign deployment of Azure IoT on Saudi sovereign cloud.

companies.name: Hitachi Lumada
companies.rationale: Hitachi Lumada provides industrial AI and IoT with active Gulf industrial customer relationships.

companies.name: Bosch Connected Industry
companies.rationale: Bosch Connected Industry provides factory IoT and analytics with strong Gulf automotive and industrial presence.
companies.contact: Bosch VP Connected Industry, Middle East

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Digital
partners.current: Largest industrial IoT deployment in Saudi Arabia; Aramco Industrial Internet platform; real-time monitoring of 6,000+ wells
partners.upgrade: Aramco IIoT platform commercialised as national Saudi IIoT standard; technology transfer to national platform; Aramco as anchor industrial reference customer

partners.name: SABIC / Industrial Operations
partners.current: Some IIoT deployment; predictive maintenance trials
partners.upgrade: SABIC as second anchor customer; SABIC petrochemical process data on Saudi platform; SABIC-KFUPM industrial AI research

partners.name: SDAIA
partners.current: National data governance; industrial data standards
partners.upgrade: National IIoT data standard; Saudi Industrial Data Sovereignty Framework; SDAIA certification for industrial IoT platforms

partners.name: Elm Company / Digital Platforms
partners.current: Government digital platforms; secure data management
partners.upgrade: Elm as Saudi IIoT platform operator; government industrial sector (Saudi Water Authority, SEC) as public sector IIoT demand
